extra.reasoning: File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts_blind/item-580239d39a17.txt. The property is the invariant G(opted_out -> not sent). The only transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (on send_attempt), which is guarded by `not opted_out`, so sent can only become true when opted_out is false. The only transition that sets opted_out:=true is t1 (on optout), and t1 atomically sets sent:=false in the same update. While opted_out is true, no enabled transition can set sent true: t2 is disabled (guard not opted_out fails), t3 sets sent:=false, t4 sets sent:=false, and t1 keeps sent:=false. Inductive argument: the initial state (opted_out=false) satisfies the invariant vacuously; from any state satisfying it, every event preserves it (either opted_out stays false, or it becomes/remains true with sent forced false). No-op events leave state unchanged and thus preserve it. Hence on all reachable executions (including up to 10 events) the invariant never breaks. Property holds; no counterexample exists.
